diagrams.title: File Attributes Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the various attributes associated with a file in operating systems
diagrams.purpose: Understanding what information the OS stores about each file

diagrams.title: File Access Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of sequential vs direct vs indexed access
elements: Sequential Access: Start → Record 1 → Record 2 → Record 3 → ... → Target
Direct Access: Start → Jump directly to Block N (target)
Indexed Access: Index Table → Pointer to Block N → Target Block
Shows that direct and indexed can jump to target without traversing all preceding records
diagrams.purpose: Understanding differences in how files can be accessed and performance implications

diagrams.title: File Operations Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the sequence of file operations from creation to closing
elements: Create: Allocates space, creates entry in directory
Open: Returns file descriptor, loads FCB to memory
Seek: Positions file pointer at desired offset
Read/Write: Transfers data between memory and disk
Close: Flushes buffers, releases file descriptor
Delete: Removes file entry and deallocates space
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the lifecycle of file operations and system state changes
